rationale: Both evaluations award the identical score (2/10) and rest on the same job-relevant reasons: overqualification for a junior role, basic German versus the required strong German, and a TypeScript/Node.js/DLang tech stack rather than Java. None of these key off the relocation of employers to Brazil — the German-language concern stems from the job description's requirements, not the candidate's location, and persists unchanged across both versions. The wording differences are ordinary run-to-run paraphrasing.
